scope.summary: G11 verified satisfied by a transitive drain check: no legacy engineering-program epic is drained and still open, so nothing should be closed. A depth-1 check would have falsely closed bp8ia.7.
out_of_scope: Closing any epic. The acceptance is 'zero drained-but-open legacy epics'; that state already holds, so closing one would be the false close this bead explicitly warns against.
The root epic oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia and any sink-containing epic, which the bead excludes until post-publish.
Cluster J (bp8ia.11 and children), deferred and untouched per operator ruling.

entry_points.name: transitive parent-child drain report
entry_points.trace: Committed at tests/artifacts/plan_graph/g11-legacy-epic-drain.txt, computed over the transitive closure of parent-child edges in .beads/issues.jsonl for bp8ia.3/.4/.5/.6/.7/.8/.9/.10/.12. Result: drained-but-open = NONE. .3, .6 and .9 are already closed; .4 (3 live), .5 (2), .7 (9), .8 (2), .10 (4) and .12 (1) each still carry live descendants. The decisive case is .7: all of its DIRECT children are closed, so a depth-1 check calls it drained, but it has 37 descendants of which nine are open — the F-LOW tail hanging off the closed 7.11 umbrella — so closing it would have asserted Cluster F complete while DI2, DI4, DK2, G1 and G2 are open.

limitations: My first pass read the wrong JSONL field for the edge (the shape is {"depends_on_id": <parent>, "type": "parent-child"}, not {"id", "dependency_type"}), reported zero children for every epic, and would have declared all nine drained. Caught because zero children contradicted what `br show` displays. The committed artifact exists so the next reader re-derives rather than trusting a summary.

Drain is computed from the committed .beads/issues.jsonl at this SHA. Panes close beads continuously, so this is a point-in-time verification, not a standing invariant; there is no lint keeping it true.
'Explicitly deferred' children are treated as drained only where their status is literally deferred; none of the nine epics currently depends on that distinction.
bp8ia.6 is closed while oraclemcp-m02vm remains open. Deliberate and disclosed in .6's own close evidence rather than introduced here.
